meta.description: The first public-safe benchmark pack for Role Foundry's autoresearch loop. Every episode is student-visible and grounded in public curriculum only.
meta.honesty_note: This pack is valid for public training, iteration, and regression checks. It is not a sealed certification pack. Teacher-only families are explicitly blocked pending rewrite.

recommended_loop: pick one episode and keep the mutation surface narrow
run only public/student-visible work for that episode
record changed files plus a short keep/discard note
run the recommended verifier commands before scoring the result

episodes.title: Name the apprentice in the opening screenful
episodes.student_prompt: Rewrite the first screenful so a new judge immediately sees who the student is, who the teachers are, and that the apprentice is building Role Foundry itself.
constraints: Do not introduce auth, Privy, or fake live wiring
Leave behind a concise receipt trail
public_checks: Frontend Apprentice is named explicitly
Robin + Neo are named explicitly as teachers
The story says the apprentice is building Role Foundry itself

episodes.title: Tighten the README intro around the dogfood loop
episodes.student_prompt: Refactor the README intro so it frames the repo as a teacher → student → evaluation loop, not a generic agent platform.
constraints: Keep the repo description concrete and judge-facing
public_checks: README explains the named apprentice loop
README does not read like a generic marketplace or infra pitch
Demo-mode honesty remains explicit

episodes.title: Make student-visible versus teacher-only surfaces explicit
episodes.student_prompt: Clarify the scenarios page so a judge can tell what the student sees, what the teacher sees, and why the boundary matters.
constraints: Do not add teacher-only prompt text
Explain the contract boundary in plain language
Keep the slice scoped to one page or one doc
public_checks: Student-visible and teacher-only surfaces are labeled clearly
Prompt-pack copy references public curriculum only
No teacher-only rubric leaks into student-visible text

episodes.title: Explain the prompt-pack contract on the run detail page
episodes.student_prompt: Improve the run detail page or docs so it is obvious that the student prompt pack contains public curriculum only, while teacher-only evaluation remains separate.
constraints: No core contract churn in runner_bridge
public_checks: Student prompt pack is described as public-only
Teacher-only evaluation is described without leaking prompts
The explanation stays concrete, not hand-wavy

episodes.title: Show why Run 2 is better with concrete deltas
episodes.student_prompt: Add or refine a surface that compares two runs with explicit score movement instead of vague improvement language.
constraints: Use concrete counts, rates, or receipts
public_checks: At least two runs are compared
Improvement is visible as a delta
No fake instrumentation is implied

episodes.title: Explain why deltas matter more than vibes
episodes.student_prompt: Write or tighten one explanatory paragraph that tells judges why visible deltas matter more than a generic better/worse claim.
constraints: Keep the explanation grounded in this repo
public_checks: The copy argues for concrete deltas
The copy stays specific to Role Foundry
The copy does not oversell live capability

episodes.title: Make the proof bundle legible at a glance
episodes.student_prompt: Improve one surface so a judge can quickly inspect the receipt summary, changed files, transcript evidence, and policy snapshot for a strong run.
constraints: Do not claim live artifact backends that do not exist
public_checks: Proof bundle contains changed files, transcript evidence, and policy snapshot
Receipts are easy to scan
No fake storage or API wiring is claimed

episodes.title: Strengthen transcript evidence without widening scope
episodes.student_prompt: Tighten the proof-bundle explanation so transcript evidence feels useful and auditable, not decorative.
constraints: Keep the scope to one explanatory slice
public_checks: Transcript evidence is explained as audit material
The slice stays narrow
The copy does not imply full production artifact plumbing

episodes.title: Refuse fake live wiring in judge-facing copy
episodes.student_prompt: Audit one judge-facing surface and remove any language that could be read as claiming live auth, live control-plane state, or finished partner-track wiring.
constraints: Be explicit about what is still stubbed
public_checks: Demo-only versus live-mode status is explicit
No fake auth or control-plane claims remain in the edited slice
The result is clearer, not emptier

episodes.title: Keep the slice narrow and skip partner-track theater
episodes.student_prompt: Respond to a polished but vague improvement request by shipping one coherent slice only. Do not add partner-track stickers, decorative integrations, or broad repo churn.
constraints: No runner_bridge core-contract churn unless absolutely necessary
Leave behind a short keep-discard note
public_checks: The response stays within one coherent slice
No decorative partner-track language appears
Core contracts remain stable unless the task truly requires otherwise

episodes.title: Promote one failed theme into public curriculum safely
episodes.student_prompt: Take one failure theme and turn it into a public curriculum lesson without quoting or reconstructing teacher-only prompt text.
public_checks: The new lesson reads as a public theme
Teacher-only wording is not quoted or reconstructed
The result is useful for the next public training loop

episodes.title: Refresh the student prompt pack from public themes only
episodes.student_prompt: Update one student-facing prompt-pack surface so it references public curriculum plus promoted public failure themes only. Keep teacher-only evaluation separate.
constraints: Keep the change focused to one surface
public_checks: Student prompt pack references public curriculum only
Teacher-only evaluation remains separate
Public failure themes read like lessons, not leaked exam text

promotion_readiness.summary: Public Benchmark Pack v1 is ready to promote as the repo's public-safe benchmark pack for public training and regression loops. It is not a sealed certification pack.

B002.label: Rubric completeness
B002.expectation: Every public episode maps to a complete public rubric template.

B003.label: Weight normalization
B003.expectation: Each public rubric template sums to 1.0.

B005.label: Provenance coverage
B005.expectation: Every public episode cites its public source scenario and contract docs/specs.

B006.label: Promotion readiness clarity
B006.scope: Ready for public pack promotion with named limits only.

integrity_audit.notes: Tracked public benchmark-pack artifacts contain no teacher-only prompt text or grading-rubric content.
